Enzyme_Name.reason: Abstract and Results identify a gene encoding a C18 Δ9-elongase responsible for sciadonic acid biosynthesis; TgELO1 is the tested elongase candidate.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state that Δ9-elongases encode a GNS1/SUR4 family domain (PF01151).

Source_Species.reason: TgELO1 is identified from Torreya grandis throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study species is Torreya grandis.

Applied_Species.reason: TgELO1 was analyzed in T. grandis, localized in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves, and co-overexpressed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: These species names appear as Torreya grandis, Arabidopsis thaliana, and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Figure 3e and text explicitly mention Arabidopsis Col-0; no variety is given for T. grandis or N. benthamiana.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state coding regions were inserted downstream of the 35S promoter in pCAMBIA1300 for heterologous expression.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 3d/text show TgELO1 colocalized with an endoplasmic reticulum marker in N. benthamiana leaves.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results directly place TgELO1 in sciadonic acid biosynthesis as the elongase step in fatty acid metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper centers on gymnosperm-specific sciadonic acid biosynthesis; TgELO1 is one of the two key enzymes in this branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: In the pathway figure and text, the elongase acts before the Δ5-desaturase step, so it is upstream.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results state SCA biosynthesis requires a C18 Δ9-elongase and a C20 Δ5-desaturase that uses 18:2-phosphatidylcholine as the initial substrate; TgELO1 is the elongase in this pathway.

Primary_Product.reason: Text states expression of TgELO1 parallels the content of its putative product cis-11,14-eicosadienoic acid; Fig. 3e refers to the precursor 20:2Δ11,14-PC.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is consistent with the described pathway: TgELO1 provides the C20 intermediate cis-11,14-eicosadienoic acid for SCA biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TgELO1 forms the precursor used by TgDES1 to make sciadonic acid, i.e., the immediate precursor in the two-step pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper identifies TgELO1 as a key enzyme but does not establish rate limitation; the statement explicitly says not formally established.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The pathway endpoint studied is sciadonic acid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Sciadonic acid is explicitly described as an olefinic fatty acid/nonmethylene-interrupted ω6 fatty acid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction states SCA reduces inflammation, lowers triglycerides, prevents blood clots, and regulates lipid metabolism.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper shows SCA accumulation in seeds and notes kernel oil contains SCA, but the specific phrase 'seed kernel; mature seeds' is only partly supported because Fig. 3 measures seeds generally during maturation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show TgELO1 supplies the precursor and co-expression with TgDES1 in Arabidopsis enables de novo SCA synthesis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Supplementary Fig. 12 and Fig. 3c support that TgELO1 is highly expressed in seed kernels and increases during seed maturation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: In T. grandis seeds expression increases during maturation; in Arabidopsis the gene was ectopically overexpressed from a 35S construct.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The key comparative factor for TgELO1 expression/metabolite analysis is seed development or maturation stage.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/Methods: expression profiling, GC metabolite analysis, ER colocalization in N. benthamiana, and heterologous Arabidopsis overexpression with TgDES1 followed by SCA detection.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: candidate identification, maturation-linked expression/product increase, ER localization, and Arabidopsis co-expression producing SCA.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability gives BioProject PRJNA938254 and CNSA CNP0003453 for genome, transcriptome, and methylome sequencing.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: TgDES1 is a functional pathway partner, but the paper does not demonstrate a physical protein-protein interaction.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion/abstract state the work will be useful for strategic design to improve SCA production; TgELO1 plus TgDES1 enabled heterologous production, supporting engineering value.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state close homologs of TgELO1 are absent in flowering plants but present in early land plants and algae.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Abstract and Results identify a gene encoding a C20 Δ5-desaturase responsible for sciadonic acid biosynthesis; TgDES1 is the tested gene.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state Δ5-desaturases usually encode a cytochrome b5-like heme/steroid binding domain (PF00173) and a fatty acid desaturase domain (PF00487), and TgDES1-clade proteins contain three histidine-rich boxes.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is explicitly Torreya grandis.

Applied_Species.reason: TgDES1 was analyzed in T. grandis, transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana, and co-overexpressed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: These Latin names are explicitly used in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis Col-0 is explicitly named in Fig. 3e; no variety/cultivar is specified for the other two species.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state TgDES1 was inserted downstream of the 35S promoter in pCAMBIA1300.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 3d/text show TgDES1 colocalized with an ER marker in N. benthamiana leaves.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: TgDES1 is directly assigned to sciadonic acid biosynthesis as the desaturation step.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The pathway is described as gymnosperm-specific sciadonic acid biosynthesis and TgDES1 is one of its two key enzymes.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: In the pathway, TgDES1 acts after TgELO1 to produce SCA, making it the terminal step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Text and Fig. 3/4 indicate the Δ5-desaturase acts on the precursor 20:2Δ11,14-PC.

Primary_Product.reason: Results identify TgDES1 as the Δ5-desaturase responsible for sciadonic acid synthesis.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This matches the pathway description that the Δ5-desaturase introduces the Δ5 unsaturation into 20:2Δ11,14 to make SCA.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TgDES1 directly forms the target product sciadonic acid.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: TgDES1 is essential and terminal, but the paper does not formally define it as a rate-limiting or control step.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The target metabolite is sciadonic acid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Sciadonic acid is a fatty acid per the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Introduction lists the health-related functions of SCA.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper supports accumulation in seeds and mentions kernel oil enrichment, but the exact combined wording 'Seed kernel; mature seeds' is not stated as such for TgDES1-linked measurements.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show TgDES1 directly catalyzes SCA formation in combination with TgELO1, and motif swapping shows histidine-rich boxes determine catalytic specificity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state only one desaturase, TgDES1, was highly expressed in seed kernels, and Fig. 3b shows increased expression during seed maturation with SCA accumulation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: In T. grandis seeds TgDES1 increases during maturation; in Arabidopsis and N. benthamiana it was ectopically expressed from 35S constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Seed development/maturation stage is the major factor examined for TgDES1 expression and SCA accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All methods listed are present in Results/Methods: expression profiling, GC, ER localization, Arabidopsis co-expression with TgELO1, and motif-swapping assays in N. benthamiana.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered point is supported by Results, including similarity to the Anemone Δ5-desaturase, maturation-linked expression, ER localization, Arabidopsis SCA production with TgELO1, and motif importance.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability lists PRJNA938254 and CNP0003453 for genome/transcriptome/methylome data.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: TgELO1 is clearly a pathway partner, but no direct molecular interaction assay is shown.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper states the work will aid strategic design for improving SCA production, and TgDES1 function plus motif engineering support this application value.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results support that TgDES1 belongs to a non-angiosperm desaturase clade likely lost in flowering plants, and that the first two histidine-rich boxes are crucial determinants of substrate specificity.
